title: Martine Barrat, a photographer without fear or limits
subhead: At 93, the artist has lost none of her vitality or curiosity. She lives at the legendary Hotel Chelsea in New York, where she remains one of the last year-round artist residents. Today, her work is the subject of a retrospective at the Rencontres d'Arles photography festival.

title: Iran strikes Gulf neighbors after new US attacks
Lindsey Graham, Republican hawk who became staunch Trump loyalist, has died
Former Spanish PM Mariano Rajoy makes racist remarks about France's football team
subhead: In an op-ed published after Spain qualified for the World Cup semifinals, Mariano Rajoy, former leader of the right-wing People's Party, said there were 'no French' in France's squad.

title: Why heatwaves disrupt our sleep – and how we can protect it
subhead: High temperatures shorten nights by affecting sleep quality, posing a threat to public health. To help combat this, one expert advises keeping up morning outings to avoid further disruptions to our body clocks.

title: How live screenings of Paris Fashion Week are making fashion more accessible
subhead: Organized by influencer Lyas, free live screenings of fashion shows on big screens toured France during Paris Men's Fashion Week. Several hundred people gathered in Marseille for the opening night.

title: Before Emile Zola, this forgotten writer was the first to defend Alfred Dreyfus
subhead: The journalist Bernard Lazare fought relentlessly to defend Captain Dreyfus and denounce the antisemitism that drove his conviction. On Sunday, France will mark its first national day commemorating the rehabilitation of the Jewish officer.

Silicon Valley's next generation feels the AI blues
subhead: In California, the rise of artificial intelligence no longer generates the unanimous enthusiasm it once did. Anxiety is mounting, especially among students and engineers, as jobs have been cut and democratic risks have grown more threatening.

title: Is air conditioning good or bad? Join this (fake) group chat for (real) analysis
subhead: Each time a new heatwave hits, the question of air conditioning and its environmental impacts crops up in the public debate. Here are the key points to help you understand what is at stake.
byline: Lara Pino Lerro and  Romain Imbach

title: How Ukraine is turning Crimea, the Russians' Riviera, into a battlefield
subhead: The annexed Ukrainian peninsula, which Vladimir Putin sees as his crowning jewel, has descended into chaos as Kyiv ramps up attacks on the territory. Shocked tourists and residents express their distress as shortages worsen.
